title: blazewrit harness — Reflect step structured_output
description: STEP 10 REFLECT — post-flow learning extraction (README legacy/steps/reflect). Runs ONLY on classification completed|abandoned; suspended => Reflect NOT executed, so this schema is never emitted for a suspended flow. M1 SHAPE (grammar-constrained generation): the 4 required substantive sections (what_worked / what_failed / unexpected / patterns_discovered) are forced present; minLength is the SCHEMA-SIDE substance FLOOR (the legacy 'Reflect structure check hook'), but the irreducible 'is it actually substantive / non-filler' judgment is NOT closable by grammar — it is an M2/floor concern (G6 review). §5 STORAGE LAW: Tier 1 raw Reflect output persists to Postgres step_runs jsonb (a RowRef self-id, NOT a flow-history file — legacy '.blazewrit/flow-history/<id>.json' is PRE-§5 and translated to a row ref). Tier 2 promotion is the SOLE legit file output: '.claude/rules/<topic>.md' (RulesFilePath, a Claude Code INPUT). based_on / prior-run refs = RowRefs. Reflect has NO reviewer and NO next-step (terminal): no DeclaredNextStep. Boundary: Reflect learns only — it does NOT change decisions (Decide), code (Implement), or write CLAUDE.md (User/Tier 3).

step.description: Step discriminant. Always 'reflect' for this schema.

result.$comment: P1 RESULT DISCRIMINANT. Reuses the Investigate compatibility_verdict.result pattern (discriminated enum + per-result routing). 'skipped_suspended' is NOT here: suspended never reaches Reflect, so that value is an orchestrator-internal control signal, never emitted in this structured_output (README Result Enum note).
result.description: P1 RESULT ENUM & BRANCHES. The primary discriminant driving the orchestrator routing table. 'completed_reflection' = 4 sections substance-pass + Tier archive done (success branch). 'reflect_incomplete' = cap 3 exhausted, substance floor not passed (Cap-Exhaustion terminal; carries incomplete_details). 'precondition_escalated' = input-precondition missing/malformed (P8) → escalate. 'archive_escalated' = primary Tier 1 Postgres archive failure → escalate. NOTE: 'skipped_suspended' (completion_status=suspended) is NOT emitted here — Reflect does not execute on suspended, so that is an orchestrator-internal control signal outside this structured_output.

self_ref.$comment: §5 Tier 1 RAW = Postgres. The Postgres step_runs row id this Reflect output persists to — NOT a '.blazewrit/flow-history/<id>.json' file (legacy prose, PRE-§5).
self_ref.description: §5 STORAGE LAW. Postgres step_runs row id under which this Reflect output (Tier 1 RAW) persists. Replaces the legacy '.blazewrit/flow-history/<id>.json' archive path — Tier 1 raw is a ROW, not a file. Auto-archived (kept) on flow completion/abandonment via Postgres retention, not a filesystem write.

classification.$comment: README 'Reflect 분류'. Only completed|abandoned reach Reflect; suspended => Reflect NOT executed (no output), so it is NOT a valid value here.
classification.description: README Reflect-classification. The terminal classification of the reflected flow. ONLY 'completed' (all steps normal) or 'abandoned' (blocked / no_op / user abandonment / RETRY_EXHAUSTED) run Reflect. 'suspended' (NEEDS_CONTEXT or active-flow preempted) does NOT execute Reflect and is therefore EXCLUDED from this enum — grammar makes it impossible to emit a Reflect output for a suspended flow.

based_on.$comment: §5 — references to the whole flow's step_runs (Triage..Verify). All RowRefs, never file paths.
based_on.description: §5 BASED-ON. RowRefs to the flow's upstream step_runs that this learning was extracted from (Triage → … → Verify, plus the Verify result). Every value is a Postgres row id (RowRef), never a file path. flow_ref points at the flows row; step_refs lists the individual step_runs rows reflected over.

flow_ref.description: RowRef to the flows row being reflected on.

step_refs.description: RowRefs to the flow's step_runs rows (Triage..Verify) the learning draws on. At least one.

verify_result.description: The flow's Verify outcome that conditioned this reflection.

what_worked.$comment: Required substantive section #1. minLength = SCHEMA-SIDE substance floor (structure-check hook). 'is it substantive / non-filler' is the irreducible G6 judgment, NOT grammar-closable.
what_worked.description: REQUIRED SECTION 1/4 (README structure check). Techniques, tools, approaches that SUCCEEDED. Each finding carries a source_tool provenance (R26 floor) so the learning is attributable to where it was observed in the flow. minItems + per-finding minLength is the schema-side substance floor; whether the text is genuinely substantive vs filler is the irreducible floor (G6).

what_failed.description: REQUIRED SECTION 2/4. What did NOT work and WHY. Same provenance + substance-floor contract as what_worked.

unexpected.description: REQUIRED SECTION 3/4. Surprises, edge cases, assumptions proven wrong. Same contract.

patterns_discovered.$comment: Required substantive section #4. Each pattern carries occurrence_count (CountClaim, R23) — observed N times; 3+ across flows triggers Tier 2 promotion.
patterns_discovered.description: REQUIRED SECTION 4/4. Recurring observations worth tracking. Each pattern additionally carries an occurrence_count (CountClaim, R23) — how many times the pattern has been observed across flows, MEASURED from the prior-run comparison, not a fabricated integer — because '3+ times' is the Tier 2 promotion trigger and that threshold must be backed by a counted command (e.g. a query over prior step_runs). promotion_eligible is the model's SHAPE-level signal that the threshold was met; the actual promotion act is recorded in tier2_promotions.

prior_learning_comparison.$comment: M3 DEGRADE — reads prior runs (legacy '.blazewrit/flow-history/', now Postgres rows). On the FIRST flow there are no priors => Omitted branch, NOT an empty/over-typed value (R22).
prior_learning_comparison.description: M3 DEGRADE (measured | cold_start | Omitted). Pass 3 prior-learning comparison: reads PRIOR flow runs (legacy '.blazewrit/flow-history/' → §5 Postgres step_runs rows) and compares this flow's learning against them. THREE first-class branches: (1) 'measured' = priors existed and were compared (carries prior_run_refs + deltas); (2) 'cold_start' = NO prior history existed (first-ever run, or no matching Tier 2 rule) — this is a LEGITIMATE baseline-establishing outcome, NOT an absence/escalate: prior history absence is the normal first-run state, so this run establishes the baseline (principle 3: empty history is a legal result, not a missing tool); (3) Omitted = the prior-run query itself FAILED/timed-out (a real tool failure, reason tool_absent/timeout). cold_start (no priors yet) is distinct from Omitted (the lookup tool broke) — never collapse the legitimate first-run into a tool-failure Omitted, and never emit a null or empty array masquerading as 'compared to nothing'.

prior_run_refs.description: RowRefs to the prior step_runs/flows rows consulted (the §5 translation of reading flow-history files).

deltas.description: How this flow's learning differs from / confirms prior learning. Each delta is provenance-bearing.

kind.description: Relationship to prior learning: a new finding, a confirmation (recurrence — feeds occurrence_count), or a contradiction (Tier 2 prune trigger).

prior_ref.description: Optional RowRef to the specific prior step_runs row this delta relates to.

oneOf.$comment: cold_start = no prior history (first-ever run / no matching Tier 2 rule). NOT an Omitted tool-failure: it is a legitimate baseline-establishing outcome (principle 3). baseline_statement records what baseline this run sets.

baseline_statement.description: What baseline this first run establishes (e.g. 'no prior baseline — this run establishes baseline'). Substance floor: non-filler.

tier2_promotions.$comment: The SOLE legit file output: .claude/rules/<topic>.md (RulesFilePath). Dedup: found=>append, not-found=>create. Append-only, never rewrite.
tier2_promotions.description: 3-Tier distillation Tier 2 (Curated). Records each pattern promoted to '.claude/rules/<topic>.md' — the ONE workflow output that stays a FILE (RulesFilePath, a Claude Code INPUT; Postgres cannot inject it). May be empty (no pattern hit the 3+ threshold this flow). DEDUP RULE (README): before writing, Reflect searches existing rule files for the same pattern → found ⇒ write_mode 'append' (append evidence to the existing file) / not-found ⇒ write_mode 'create' (new file). Append-only — never rewrite. Each promotion's pattern_ref ties back to the patterns_discovered entry that justified it.

items.$comment: topic + pattern_ref are always required. rules_file / occurrence_count / deduped_against are required only for an actually-written promotion (append|create); a 'deferred' item (Tier 2 write contention/IO failure) carries NO successfully-written rules_file and instead records the pending state — see the allOf branch below. The primary learning is preserved in Tier 1 either way (degrade, not escalate).

rules_file.$comment: §5 SOLE file-path exception — a real on-disk .claude/rules/<topic>.md path. Present only when the write succeeded (write_mode append|create), absent for deferred.
rules_file.description: The Tier 2 file written. RulesFilePath ('.claude/rules/<topic>.md') — the single file output in the whole harness. Required for append|create; OMITTED for a deferred (failed/contended) write.

write_mode.description: Dedup/write outcome: 'append' = identical pattern already had a rule file, evidence appended (append-only); 'create' = no existing rule matched, new file created; 'deferred' = Tier 2 file write contended/failed (lock/IO error), so the promotion is recorded as PENDING in Tier 1 (primary learning preserved) — a degraded branch, NOT an escalate. The orchestrator retries the deferred write on next idle.

topic.description: The <topic> slug of the rule file (matches the rules_file path stem).

pattern_ref.description: Index into patterns_discovered identifying which discovered pattern was promoted.

occurrence_count.$comment: R23 — the '3+ times across flows' promotion threshold is a MEASURED count, never a bare integer. value MUST be >= 3 for a legitimate promotion (validator-enforced).
occurrence_count.description: R23 CountClaim. Times the pattern was observed across flows — the measured backing for the '3+' promotion threshold. Must be derived from the prior-run query stdout, not fabricated. M2 validator asserts value >= 3 (promotion threshold) AND value is derivable from source.raw_stdout.

deduped_against.$comment: Dedup audit: which existing rule files were searched before writing. Never create a duplicate rule.
deduped_against.description: The existing '.claude/rules/' files searched during dedup (README Dedup Rule). Empty when no rule files existed yet. M2 validator: if write_mode='create', assert no entry here matched the same pattern (no duplicate rule created).

deferred_reason.$comment: Present only when write_mode=deferred — why the Tier 2 file write could not complete (lock contention / IO error).
deferred_reason.description: Why the Tier 2 write was deferred (lock contention / IO failure). The promotion is recorded PENDING in Tier 1 (tier2_write_pending) and retried later; primary learning is not lost. Used only for write_mode=deferred.

allOf.$comment: A WRITTEN promotion (append|create) carries the real file path + measured count + dedup audit.

A DEFERRED promotion has no successfully-written file; rules_file is forbidden and deferred_reason is required. The pattern_ref still ties it to the discovered pattern so the pending write can be retried.

iterations.$comment: Internal multi-pass: max 3 iterations until 4 sections substantive. This is a COUNT of executed passes => CountClaim is overkill (not a tool-stdout count); it is a self-reported control integer bounded 1..3.
iterations.description: Internal multi-pass control. Number of refinement iterations run (Pass 1 fact collection → Pass 2 pattern extraction → Pass 3 prior comparison, looped up to max 3 until the 4 required sections are substantive). Bounded 1..3. This is an internal control integer (how many self-passes the agent ran), NOT a measurement of an external tool's stdout, so it is a plain bounded integer rather than a CountClaim.

unverified.$comment: R13 floor — KEEP polarity. Reflect may carry unverified learnings; the flag propagates. NO 'no unverified remain' forbid.
unverified.description: R13 UNVERIFIED FLAG (floor, KEEP polarity). Optional: whether some of this reflection's learnings are themselves unverified. Designed to PERSIST — NEVER forbidden true. Reflect is terminal and is NOT the unverified gate (Verify is); Reflect merely records.

incomplete_details.$comment: Cap-Exhaustion terminal (P5). Required ONLY when result=reflect_incomplete. Silent pass forbidden: the failure is reported explicitly.
incomplete_details.description: CAP-EXHAUSTION DETAIL (result=reflect_incomplete). After max 3 iterations without passing the substance floor, Reflect reports the failure explicitly instead of silently passing a hollow output. failing_sections lists which of the 4 sections stayed filler; iterations_used is the passes run (= 3 at cap); reason states why. Partial raw is still archived with status=incomplete and excluded from Tier 2 promotion.

failing_sections.description: Which of the 4 required sections failed the substance floor.

iterations_used.description: Refinement passes run before giving up (= 3 at the cap).

reason.description: Why the substance floor could not be met.

escalate.$comment: Post-flow escalate channel. Required when result ∈ {precondition_escalated, archive_escalated}. failure_origin is cap_exceeded (post-flow halt origin, NO auto-reinvoke) — Reflect runs after flow termination so it does NOT borrow the in-flow failure_origin=verify value, and does NOT add its own step value (Boundary: routing non-ownership). Reuses shared FailureOrigin $def.
escalate.description: POST-FLOW ESCALATE (result ∈ {precondition_escalated, archive_escalated}). Carries failure_origin (always 'cap_exceeded' — the orchestrator-level halt origin, NO auto-reinvoke; Reflect runs post-termination and has no in-flow re-entry, so no ping-pong loop exists) and a reason. precondition_escalated covers missing/invalid completion_status, malformed (flow_id, flow_type), or a completed-flow step row that is malformed. archive_escalated covers Tier 1 (Postgres) archive failure — the primary output could not be persisted, so silent pass is forbidden.

failure_origin.$comment: Constrained to cap_exceeded — the only post-flow halt origin Reflect emits (reuses shared FailureOrigin enum, no new value invented).
failure_origin.description: Post-flow halt origin. Always 'cap_exceeded' (halt+escalate, NO auto-reinvoke). Drawn from the shared FailureOrigin enum but Reflect only ever emits cap_exceeded.

reason.description: Why Reflect escalated (precondition missing/malformed, or Tier 1 archive failure).

allOf.$comment: completed_reflection => the 4 substantive sections + Pass-3 comparison are required; the escalate/incomplete carriers are forbidden.
reflect_incomplete => incomplete_details required (Cap-Exhaustion); sections are NOT unconditionally required (some failed the floor); no escalate.
precondition_escalated / archive_escalated => escalate object required; no normal sections / incomplete_details obligation.

Finding.$comment: One substantive learning item. R26 floor: source_tool REQUIRED. source_tool = the OBSERVATION-ORIGIN tool (which tool surfaced this learning), NOT the forbidden per-section cross-flow artifact citation (P5 over-reach). minLength on text = schema-side substance floor.
Finding.description: A single learning finding inside a required section. text is the observation (minLength substance floor — the structure-check hook's machine-checkable part). source_tool (R26 irreducible floor) names WHICH TOOL surfaced this observation — REQUIRED so no learning is unsourced. NOTE (P5): this is the observation-origin tool, DISTINCT from the per-section artifact citation Reflect does NOT require (cross-flow provenance Reflect cannot supply — Investigate/Ground's job). step_ref optionally pins the finding to the specific step_runs row it came from (optional, not the forbidden mandatory citation).

text.description: The substantive observation. minLength 24 is the schema-side substance FLOOR; non-filler is the irreducible G6 judgment.

step_ref.description: Optional RowRef to the step_runs row this finding was observed in.

ReflectionSection.$comment: Wrapper for the 3 narrative sections (what_worked/what_failed/unexpected). minItems 1 = at least one finding; empty section = non-substantive (structure-check hook).
ReflectionSection.description: A required substantive section = a non-empty list of provenance-bearing findings. minItems 1 is the schema-side substance floor (an empty section fails the structure check). Each finding's minLength text is the per-item substance floor.

DiscoveredPattern.$comment: patterns_discovered item. occurrence_count = CountClaim (R23): measured times-observed backing the 3+ promotion threshold.
DiscoveredPattern.description: A recurring pattern worth tracking. text is the pattern statement (substance floor). occurrence_count (R23 CountClaim) is the MEASURED number of times the pattern has been observed across flows — the count that drives Tier 2 promotion; it must be derived from the prior-run query stdout, not invented. promotion_eligible is the SHAPE-level signal (occurrence_count.value >= 3); the actual promotion is in tier2_promotions. source_tool (R26) required.

text.description: The pattern statement. minLength 24 substance floor.

occurrence_count.description: R23 CountClaim — times observed across flows (the 3+ promotion threshold backing). Measured from the prior-run query, M2-validated as derivable from raw_stdout.

promotion_eligible.description: true if occurrence_count.value >= 3 (Tier 2 promotion threshold met). M2 validator asserts this boolean is consistent with occurrence_count.value.
